_meta.description: Comic series (Mature line). Canonical source of truth. Descriptions are the approved Canonical Public Content (premise-level; endings excluded). Tiers per Appendix A2.

series.title: I Ride for Them
series.synopsis: In a 2052 London so overpopulated that childbirth has been outlawed, an engineer builds a black-market virtual-reality program where people can still experience birth — and entrusts it to Joy, the AI midwife she creates. When a virus corrupts the program and abandons newborn digital children in a dangerous virtual world, Joy becomes their protector.

cover.alt: I Ride for Them #1 cover art by Giulia Gualazzi: a woman in scrubs cradles an infant while holding a pistol against a neon city.

issues.title: I Ride for Them #1
issues.synopsis: The story begins — the program, and the AI midwife Joy who watches over the children within it.

cover.alt: I Ride for Them #1 cover by Giulia Gualazzi.

issues.title: I Ride for Them #2
issues.synopsis: Hunted through the virtual world, Joy fights for the children in her care.

cover.alt: I Ride for Them #2 cover by Giulia Gualazzi: Joy at a sink facing a ghostly reflection.

series.title: Society of Killers
series.tagline: They're going to make you an offer you should refuse.
series.synopsis: The Kellerman Institute — the world's oldest secret academy, founded in 1396 — recruits and trains elite psychopaths to become perfect killers. Inside its closed world, a class of subjects led by the coldly composed Astrid St. James navigates its tests, rivalries, and rituals.

cover.alt: Society of Killers #1 cover: a gentleman in a bowler hat extends a red calling card across a desk.

issues.title: Society of Killers #1
issues.synopsis: Inside the Kellerman Institute, a new class of subjects begins its training.

cover.alt: Society of Killers #1 cover by Giulia Gualazzi.

issues.title: Society of Killers #2
issues.synopsis: The subjects are tested against one another, and rivalries sharpen.

cover.alt: Society of Killers #2 cover: an ensemble of stylish, armed characters.
